feature: Inventory and dependency graph: OpenCost integration / cost
user_story: As an operator, I can understand and act on OpenCost integration cost posture without leaving Mayyam.
ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for OpenCost integration: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Discover OpenCost integration, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the cost pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for OpenCost integration.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for OpenCost integration.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Kubernetes Cost Allocation OpenCost integration inventory with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: opencost-integration_cost_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for OpenCost integration cost that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for OpenCost integration; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for OpenCost integration.
cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for OpenCost integration, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for OpenCost integration.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for OpenCost integration.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing OpenCost integration data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling OpenCost integration inventory broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for OpenCost integration.
acceptance_criteria: Given OpenCost integration data exists, when the cost inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: OpenCost integration connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for network cost: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Discover network cost, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the performance pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for network cost.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for network cost.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Kubernetes Cost Allocation network cost inventory with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: network-cost_performance_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for network cost performance that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for network cost; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for network cost.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for network cost recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for network cost.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for network cost.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing network cost data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling network cost inventory broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for network cost.
acceptance_criteria: Given network cost data exists, when the performance inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: network cost connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for over-requested memory: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Support read, validate, mutate, rollback, and audit workflows for over-requested memory with operational-excellence guardrails.
backend_scope: Add validated commands, dry-run support, RBAC checks, and audit records for over-requested memory.
frontend_scope: Add action menu, confirmations, diff previews, and result status for over-requested memory.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Kubernetes Cost Allocation over-requested memory operations with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: over-requested-memory_operational-excellence_operations evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for over-requested memory operational-excellence that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for over-requested memory; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for over-requested memory.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for over-requested memory recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for over-requested memory.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for over-requested memory.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing over-requested memory data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling over-requested memory operations broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for over-requested memory.
acceptance_criteria: Given over-requested memory data exists, when the operational-excellence operations workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: over-requested memory connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging
